description: LCR (Listen and Choose a Response) — ETS profile derived from 16 reference samples. Used for generation calibration and quality gating.

speaker_sentence.register: casual spoken English, contractions OK, campus/daily life
common_patterns: Didn't I just...? (negative question seeking confirmation)
I have to tell you... (preface to news)

correct_answer.key_insight: ~44% of correct answers are INDIRECT — they address the intent/emotion behind the words, not the literal question. This is the core pragmatic skill being tested.
correct_answer.register: natural spoken English, may include idioms (I'm all ears, Absolutely)

distractors.word_trap_description: Reuses a key word from the speaker sentence in a misleading context (e.g., 'library'→'reference section', 'bus'→'missed the bus', 'tell'→'told you')
distractors.off_topic_description: Grammatically correct English but completely irrelevant to the conversation context
distractors.wrong_question_type_description: Answers a different type of question than what was asked (e.g., answers 'where' when asked 'when')
distractors.key_insight: Each item should have distractors from at least 2 different types. Word traps are the most common — almost always 1 per item.

pragmatic_functions_tested.description: The communicative intent the test-taker must recognize

answer_position_distribution.note: Roughly balanced. Real ETS tests aim for even distribution.

option_length.description: All 4 options should be similar length
option_length.parallel_structure: Options should start with similar grammatical structures when possible

must_have: Speaker sentence sounds natural and spoken (not written English)
Correct answer addresses communicative INTENT, not just literal words
At least 1 word-trap distractor per item
All 4 options are grammatically correct English
Options are similar in length (within 5 words of each other)
avoid: Speaker sentences that are too formal/academic
Correct answers that are too obvious/literal
Distractors that are obviously wrong (gibberish, grammar errors)
All distractors being the same type (e.g., all word traps)
Correct answer being consistently the longest option

difficulty_calibration.easy: Direct question + direct answer (factual info exchange)
difficulty_calibration.medium: Statement implying action + indirect response needed
difficulty_calibration.hard: Negative question or emotional statement + must recognize intent behind words, idiom knowledge helpful
